details: Keep existing English URLs exactly as /tools/{english-slug}/, /tools/{hub}/, /categories/{category}/, and /. Do not add /en/ unless separately approved.
Generate localized tools as /{lang}/tools/{localized-slug}/ for hi es ru fr de it pt bn ja ko ms pl id ar bg tr sv.
Each language gets its own localized discovery homepage with local copy, localized hub links, and hreflang alternatives.
Create localized hub pages for safe high-value groups; map each hub with a stable internal hub ID and localized route.

recommendation: Use /{lang}/categories/{localized-category-slug}/ only if categories remain useful
details: Prefer localized hubs for SEO landing pages; categories can be localized as navigation/discovery pages once hub mapping is stable.
The 20 redirected/merged English source URLs must not get localized standalone pages. If requested, redirect to the localized target if available or English target.
Every active tool keeps canonicalToolId equal to current English slug. Localized route data is keyed by canonicalToolId, not by translated slug.
Store localizedSlug by lang and canonicalToolId. Generate pages only when localizationStatus is ready and route is unique for that language.
If a tool is not localized, language switch can fall back to English same-tool URL. Do not generate thin machine-fallback localized pages.
For es fr de it pt ms pl id tr sv, use local keyword phrase in the native Latin spelling, ASCII-normalized where practical for accents, lowercase, hyphenated.

recommendation: Prefer romanized slugs for technical stability
details: For ru bg, store native keyword and optional Unicode slug, but recommend romanized SEO-safe slugs to reduce sharing/encoding issues.
For hi bn, store native keyword and native-script display title, but recommend romanized slugs for stable URLs unless local SEO research proves Unicode slug value is higher.

recommendation: Prefer romanized or English-hybrid slugs initially
details: For ja ko, native keyword belongs in H1/title/meta; use romanized or English-hybrid slugs because Unicode paths are less readable when percent-encoded.

recommendation: Prefer romanized Arabic keyword slugs initially
details: For ar, store Arabic keyword and RTL content, but use romanized slugs to avoid percent-encoding/shareability problems.
If localized slugs collide within a language, add a local qualifier that preserves intent, such as text, seo, business, random, or online. Never append arbitrary numbers for indexable pages.

recommendation: Use local SERP and keyword data
details: For each canonicalToolId and language, collect local candidates from Google autocomplete, SERP titles, People Also Ask, Search Console if available, keyword tools, and competitor page language.
Reject keywords that imply a different output, risky use case, legal promise, impersonation, brand/IP target, or unavailable feature even if volume is higher.
Choose the local phrase with the strongest same-intent demand; prefer high commercial or task intent over literal translation.
Generate slug from selected keyword, remove filler words when natural, transliterate where strategy recommends, normalize to lowercase hyphen-separated route segment.
Align localized H1, title, meta title, intro first sentence, and internal anchors around the chosen local keyword without stuffing.
Require native-language review for top languages before indexable launch; LLM translation alone is not enough for final keyword choice.
Human-readable local page title, not necessarily identical to keyword.
H1 includes strongest local same-intent keyword.
SERP title localized for click intent, length-controlled per language.
Localized benefit and safe-use note, length-controlled and not a literal English translation.
Helpful intro explaining what the tool does, who it is for, and when to review output.
Short local summary for cards and internal links.
3 to 5 localized FAQs with local phrasing and no generic boilerplate.
Below-tool guide covering use cases, tips, review steps, and limitations.
Translate labels, buttons, options, placeholders, validation text, copy buttons, and mode names.
Mirror English safety posture and adapt examples for local context.
Related links use localized titles and localized URLs when available.
Hub intro, group headings, descriptions, FAQs, and related hub links localized by intent.
Every localized page canonicalizes to its own https://taptogen.com/{lang}/... URL. Real localized pages must not canonicalize to English.
Build hreflang from canonicalToolId route map. Include only available localized versions of the same tool or hub, not language homepages.
Use x-default pointing to the English same-tool URL for tool pages; for localized homepages use x-default to /.
Generate localized sitemap entries only for indexable localized pages. Optionally split by language once scale grows.
Localized noindex pages are excluded from sitemap. Redirected/removed localized routes are never included.
Set html lang to the BCP 47 code for the current page, e.g. es, fr, de, hi, bn, ar.
Set dir=rtl for ar and dir=ltr for the other target languages unless a future language requires RTL.
Keep UTF-8 across source, build output, sitemap XML, JSON-LD, meta tags, and CSV/import pipelines. Add mojibake tests.

recommendation: Do not localize noindex tools initially
details: Safest launch: localize only indexable English tools. Leave the 51 active noindex tools in English only until a separate safety review approves localized noindex pages.
If noindex tools are localized later, keep them noindex/follow, exclude from sitemap, omit from SEO hubs, and avoid promoting in discovery sections.
Do not create localized standalone pages for the 20 redirected/merged routes. Create localized redirects only if needed for user navigation.
Fields: code, label, nativeName, locale, dir, slugStrategy, enabled, indexable, fallbackLang.
Fields: canonicalToolId, lang, localizedSlug, route, status, indexable, canonicalUrl, sourceQuality, reviewer, updatedAt.
Fields: title, h1, metaTitle, metaDescription, primaryKeyword, secondaryKeywords, ogTitle, ogDescription.
Fields: intro, tagline, faqItems, guideSections, safeUseNote, examples, relatedIntro, hubDescriptions.
Input current canonicalToolId/current route/targetLang. Output same localized tool route if ready; else English same-tool; else same-language hub; homepage only as last resort.
Build alternates from route map for same canonicalToolId or hubId. Include self, English, available localized pages, and x-default.
Emit only localized pages where status=ready and indexable=true and canonicalToolId is not noindex/redirected.
Stable hubId with localized slug, title, intro, FAQs, related hub IDs, and included canonicalToolIds.
Language switch should prefer /tools/{english-slug}/, preserving user intent over language consistency.
Only if the exact tool is unavailable or intentionally not localized, use the most relevant same-language hub.
Never send to unrelated homepage unless no safe same-tool or hub alternative exists.
Fail build or audit if any language has duplicate localized slugs within tools or hubs.
Audit missing localized route mappings for every enabled language and planned tool.
For sampled and eventually all pages, switching languages must preserve canonicalToolId or documented fallback reason.
Check reciprocal hreflang, valid language codes, no alternates to missing/noindex/redirected pages, and x-default correctness.
Check localized sitemap includes only indexable localized pages, excludes noindex and redirected pages, and contains production URLs.
Check self-canonical on every real localized page and no cross-language canonical to English.
Assert English routes, slugs, tool counts, redirects, noindexToolSlugs, and sitemap exclusion logic remain unchanged.
Check localized homepage, hubs, categories, related tools, footer, and language switch links for broken URLs and noindex leaks.
Measure generated page count, build time, memory use, sitemap size, client bundle size, and static route generation time by batch.
Current production-ready English build remains the baseline.
430 active tools times 17 localized languages, if all active tools eventually get localized pages.
379 indexable tools times 17 localized languages, if noindex tools are not localized initially.
Depends on localized hubs, category pages, homepages, legal pages, and whether noindex tools are localized.
